content:
- Resumed `test-harness-binary-check-panics` at TO BE VERIFIED, ran deep skeptical verification with broad parallel scans, and changed the plan by requiring an explicit missing-path contract test in `src/test_harness/binaries.rs`.
- Flipped task marker to NOW EXECUTE, added `require_binary_missing_path_returns_invalid_input` (asserts `Err(HarnessError::InvalidInput(_))`), executed compile + focused tests, and ran required gates successfully: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`.
- Updated task file to done (`<status>done</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>`), checked acceptance boxes, added execution evidence, appended a new learning to `AGENTS.md`, and ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`; next immediate step is commit all tracked/untracked changes with the mandated message format.

- Finalized task closure and committed all changes as `7d34e5c` with message `task finished test-harness-binary-check-panics: add missing-path harness error regression and close stale panic report`.
- Commit includes code/test/task/docs artifacts plus `.ralph` updates from task switch and evidence logs; git status is clean.
- All required gates were green before commit: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, and `make lint`.

- Startup: ran .ralph/progress_read.sh for Choose-Task; no prior log output.
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and applied selection rules. Found at least one bug with <passes>false</passes>: .ralph/tasks/bugs/worker-contract-tests-assert-only-callability.md.
- Applied mandatory bug-first rule: selected that bug task before any non-bug or meta-task.
- Wrote only that path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next step for next agent window: execute the selected bug task and revalidate gates per task workflow.

- Started from `.ralph/current_task.txt` and loaded active bug task: `worker-contract-tests-assert-only-callability.md`.
- Per workflow, detected no phase marker (`TO BE VERIFIED` / `NOW EXECUTE`), so performed planning-only phase.
- Ran broad parallel exploration across worker contract tests and worker implementations (`pginfo`, `dcs`, `process`, `ha`, `api`, `debug_api`) plus state-channel semantics to identify reliable behavioral assertions.
- Wrote a detailed implementation plan into the task file with concrete per-worker assertions, sequential gate verification steps, completion workflow steps, and ended plan with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Did not execute code changes/tests yet (intentionally stopped after planning marker update, as requested).
- Next step for next turn/engineer: when seeing `TO BE VERIFIED`, run deep skeptical plan review, alter at least one plan item, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.
